author: spencer_tb

lol I was referring to glam 7

successful glam 5 (CL focus) + successful glam 6 (EL focus) = glam 7 (merge of the 2)

author: draganrakita
content:
We need hackmd to do summary of what is in glam-devnet-5, what changes are pending for glam-devnet-6 and what is left for glam-devnet-7.

imo it would be good to have Glamsterdam main hackmd, that would list other devnet hackmds

author: holgerd77
content:
Other topic, totally forgot to announce a bit here that we now have the fully Amsterdam-compatible **EthereumJS releases** out (of course still experimental, passing `bal-devnet-7` tests).

So this is an easy way to run and test certain Amsterdam features with Node.js or in-browser, this is e.g. our Twitter thread on how to test the new DUPN/SWAPN/EXCHANGE opcodes.
https://x.com/EFJavaScript/status/2062162209631854772

If you would benefit from some browser visualizations/tools/... here around some of the new EIPs here let us know, we might be able to help! 🙏

author: parithosh
content: The issue is that we're still trying to get stable glamsterdam-devnet-5. We dont want to pre-commit to exactly what goes into 6, but the focus would be to merge bal-devnets and epbs focussed glamsterdam-devnets ASAP so there's much less confusion, even if there's slower progress in sub-streams in the short term while we get there. We really need to have everyone on the same page now + client branches now, benchmarks + repricings + epbs + bal + whatever else we add to the fork.

author: draganrakita
content: Understandable, i mostly looked at EL side where we closing in on Amsterdam finish line, as all EIPs and changes on EL should be finished in next devnet.

author: parithosh
content: Yeah we should be close now, its mostly merging everything in and focussing on hardening the fork from here on. There are still a few EIPs on the CL side that might go in, it depends on how the timelines for those look and how important they are.
